tool_name: Laser Cutting Time and Assist Gas Cost Calculator — Sheet Metal Laser Process Time, Gas Consumption and Cost Check
category: Sheet Metal / Laser Cutting / Cost Engineering

inputs.name: Material Thickness
inputs.hint: Sheet or plate thickness in mm. Laser cutting range depends on laser power and material type.

inputs.name: Laser Power (at workpiece)
inputs.hint: Effective laser power delivered to the workpiece. Fiber: 1-12 kW, CO2: 0.5-6 kW. Check machine calibration log.

inputs.name: Machine Rated Laser Power
inputs.hint: Rated laser power from machine nameplate. Used for power utilization ratio calculation.

inputs.name: Programmed Cut Length
inputs.hint: Total programmed cutting path length per part from CAM system.

inputs.name: Lead-in / Lead-out Length
inputs.hint: Total lead-in and lead-out length per contour. Typically 1-5 mm per pierce.

inputs.name: Average Programmed Feed Rate
inputs.hint: Average cutting feed rate from CAM system for the programmed cutting path. Fiber laser mild steel 1mm: ~6000-12000 mm/min; 6mm: ~2000-4000 mm/min.

inputs.name: Feed Override Factor
inputs.hint: Actual feed rate as fraction of programmed. Operator override: 0.8-1.2 typical. Acceleration-limited paths may reduce effective feed.

inputs.name: Contour Complexity Factor
inputs.hint: Factor compensating for acceleration/deceleration losses on complex contours. Simple rectangles: 1.0; tight radii < 2mm: 1.3-1.5; high-detail with many small features: 2.0-3.0.

inputs.name: Number of Pierces
inputs.hint: Total number of pierce operations per part. Each closed contour typically requires one pierce + lead-in.

inputs.name: Average Pierce Time
inputs.hint: Average time per pierce operation. Fiber laser mild steel 1mm: 0.3-0.5 s; 6mm: 1-3 s; 12mm: 3-8 s. Pulsed piercing is faster than ramp piercing.
inputs.uncertainty_source: Machine OEM cutting database — pierce time variability

inputs.name: Rapid Traverse Distance
inputs.hint: Total rapid traverse (positioning) distance per part between cutting operations.

inputs.name: Average Rapid Traverse Speed
inputs.hint: Average rapid traverse speed. Typical: 30000-60000 mm/min for mid-range; up to 120000 mm/min for high-speed gantry.

inputs.name: Auxiliary Time per Part
inputs.hint: Additional time per part for loading/unloading, part separation (micro-joints), marking, and inspection.

inputs.name: Setup Time
inputs.hint: Job setup time including sheet loading, program selection, nozzle check, focus calibration, and gas purge.

inputs.name: Measured Cutting Gas Flow
inputs.hint: Directly measured gas flow rate at nozzle in normal cubic meters per hour. Required when gas_flow_mode = MEASURED_FLOW.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 5167-1:2003 — Flow measurement uncertainty ±2% for calibrated thermal mass flow meter

inputs.name: Nozzle Diameter
inputs.hint: Cutting nozzle orifice diameter. Typical: 1.0-3.0 mm for sheet metal laser cutting.

inputs.name: Assist Gas Pressure
inputs.hint: Assist gas pressure at nozzle inlet. Nitrogen: 6-20 bar; Oxygen: 0.5-6 bar; Compressed air: 4-10 bar.
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Pressure transducer class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Calibrated Flow Coefficient (Cv_nozzle)
inputs.hint: Empirical nozzle flow coefficient for CALIBRATED_NOZZLE_PRESSURE_ESTIMATE mode. Typical range: 0.1-1.5 for conical nozzles. Derived from OEM nozzle flow characterization.
inputs.uncertainty_source: Nozzle flow calibration — empirical coefficient uncertainty

inputs.name: Pierce Gas Flow Factor
inputs.hint: Gas flow multiplier during piercing vs. steady-state cutting. Pulsed piercing at high pressure: 2-4x. Ramp piercing: 1-2x.

inputs.name: Assist Gas Unit Cost
inputs.hint: Cost per normal cubic meter of assist gas. Nitrogen (liquid): 0.10-0.50 USD/Nm³; Oxygen: 0.15-0.60 USD/Nm³; Argon: 1.0-5.0 USD/Nm³; Compressed air: 0.02-0.10 USD/Nm³.

inputs.name: Machine Hour Rate
inputs.hint: Fully loaded machine hour rate including depreciation, maintenance, floor space, labor, and overhead. Typical laser cutting: 50-200 USD/h.
inputs.uncertainty_source: Cost accounting — machine hour rate estimate

inputs.name: Average Machine Electrical Power (cutting)
inputs.hint: Average electrical power consumption during cutting (including chiller, exhaust, drives, CNC, and laser power supply). Fiber 4kW: ~15-25 kW total; CO2 4kW: ~30-50 kW total.
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61000-4-30:2015 — Power measurement uncertainty Class A: ±0.5% (system-level: ±10% including auxiliary loads)

inputs.name: Standby Electrical Power
inputs.hint: Electrical power consumption in standby/idle mode (laser off, drives idle, chiller standby). Typical: 3-10 kW for fiber laser systems.

inputs.name: Electricity Unit Cost
inputs.hint: Industrial electricity cost per kWh including demand charges, taxes, and surcharges. Typical: 0.08-0.25 USD/kWh.

inputs.name: Consumable Cost per Pierce
inputs.hint: Consumable cost attributed per pierce (nozzle wear, lens contamination, protective window degradation). Approx: 0.001-0.05 USD/pierce for fiber laser.

inputs.name: Consumable Cost per Cut Meter
inputs.hint: Consumable cost per meter of cut (protective window, nozzle wear, focus lens degradation). Approx: 0.01-0.10 USD/m.

inputs.name: Scrap / Recut Allowance Factor
inputs.hint: Factor to account for scrap parts requiring re-cutting and process inefficiency. 1.0 = no scrap; 1.05 = 5% scrap rate typical.

formulas.name: Cutting Feed Rate (Effective)
assumptions: Override factor linearly scales programmed feed rate
Acceleration/deceleration effects handled by contour_complexity_factor
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Effective Cutting Path Length per Part
assumptions: Lead-in/out applied per pierce for each contour
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Pure Cutting Time per Part
assumptions: Complex contours reduce effective feed due to acceleration/deceleration
edge_cases: Effective_Feed_mm_min <= 0: Division by zero — block calculation

formulas.name: Total Pierce Time per Part
assumptions: Pierce time is constant per operation

formulas.name: Rapid Traverse Time per Part
assumptions: Constant rapid speed (acceleration/deceleration ignored)

formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.reference: ISO 14955-1 — Machine tool cycle time definition
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

assumptions: Scrap allowance applies linearly to cycle time per part
Setup is fixed per job

assumptions: Machine hour rate includes all overhead

formulas.name: Effective Volume Flow Rate at Nozzle (MEASURED_FLOW mode)
edge_cases: gas_flow_mode != 'MEASURED_FLOW': Use alternative flow determination method
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs. Return null if mode is not MEASURED_FLOW.

formulas.name: Nozzle Cross-Sectional Area
assumptions: Circular nozzle orifice
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Gas Flow Estimate via OEM Nozzle Table (OEM_NOZZLE_TABLE mode)
formulas.reference: Machine OEM nozzle flow table — empirical correlation
assumptions: Linear approximation: flow ∝ nozzle_area × pressure
Factor 0.85 is typical for conical nozzles
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs. Return null if mode is not OEM_NOZZLE_TABLE.

formulas.name: Gas Flow Estimate via Calibrated Nozzle Pressure Method (CALIBRATED_NOZZLE_PRESSURE_ESTIMATE mode)
formulas.reference: Nozzle flow calibration — ISO 5167-1:2003 orifice flow principle
assumptions: Flow is proportional to calibrated coefficient × area × pressure
Gas density and temperature effects implicitly included in calibration coefficient
edge_cases: calibrated_flow_coefficient_Nm3_h_mm2_bar is null or gas_flow_mode != 'CALIBRATED_NOZZLE_PRESSURE_ESTIMATE': Return null
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs. Return null if mode is not CALIBRATED_NOZZLE_PRESSURE_ESTIMATE.

formulas.name: Selected Cutting Gas Flow Rate
assumptions: Precedence: measured > OEM table > calibrated estimate
edge_cases: All flow determination methods return null: Flow = 0 — gas cost will be zero; verify flow mode and inputs
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs. Select the first non-null flow value in priority order: measured, OEM table, calibrated estimate. If all null, return 0.

formulas.name: Total Cutting Gas Consumption per Part
assumptions: Steady-state gas flow during entire cycle time
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Total Pierce Gas Consumption per Part
assumptions: Gas flow during piercing is pierce_gas_flow_factor × cutting flow rate
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Total Gas Consumption per Part
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

assumptions: Scrap allowance applies linearly to gas consumption

assumptions: Average electrical power during cutting is representative of total cycle

assumptions: Standby power draw during setup time

assumptions: Consumable wear is linear per pierce and per meter of cut

assumptions: Scrap allowance applies to consumable consumption

formulas.name: Total Manufacturing Cost per Part
formulas.expression: Total_Cost_Part = Machine_Cost / (job_part_count * scrap_recut_allowance_factor) + Gas_Cost_Part + Consumable_Cost_Part + (Cut_Energy_kWh * electricity_unit_cost_per_kWh) / (job_part_count * scrap_recut_allowance_factor)
assumptions: Total machine and energy cost is distributed across all parts including scrap
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

feed_rate_positive.error_msg: Average programmed feed rate must be positive.

rapid_speed_positive.error_msg: Average rapid traverse speed must be positive.

rated_power_positive.error_msg: Machine rated laser power must be positive.

laser_power_not_exceed_rated.error_msg: Laser power at workpiece cannot exceed machine rated laser power.

gas_pressure_positive.error_msg: Assist gas pressure must be positive.

machine_hour_rate_positive.error_msg: Machine hour rate must be positive.

electricity_cost_positive.error_msg: Electricity unit cost must be positive.

assist_gas_cost_positive.error_msg: Assist gas unit cost must be positive.

laser_safety_required.error_msg: Laser safety verification (IEC 60825-1) is required before proceeding. Safety audit must confirm laser enclosure, interlocks, and beam path shielding.

machine_parameters_required.error_msg: Machine parameter table must be verified against current machine setup.

gas_flow_mode_measured_requires_flow_val.error_msg: Measured gas flow mode requires a valid measured gas flow rate.

gas_flow_mode_calibrated_requires_coeffi.error_msg: Calibrated nozzle pressure estimate mode requires a valid calibrated flow coefficient.

smart_warnings.message: Low power utilization ratio (< 40%): Laser is operating well below rated power. Consider using a lower-rated laser machine for better energy efficiency or verify that the cutting parameters are optimal for the material/thickness.
Effective feed rate < 100 mm/min: Extremely slow cutting. Verify material thickness and laser power combination. High risk of excessive heat input, dross formation, and wide kerf.
Total production time exceeds 8 hours: Job spans multiple shifts. Verify machine availability and shift planning. Consider increasing shift utilization for cost efficiency.

Argon assist gas cost > 2 USD/Nm³: Argon is significantly more expensive than nitrogen or compressed air. For laser cutting applications, verify that argon is required (e.g., titanium cutting) rather than a less expensive alternative.
Gas cost exceeds 30% of total job cost: Assist gas consumption is a dominant cost driver. Review gas flow rate, nozzle condition, and consider alternative gas types or lower pressure settings.

Contour complexity factor > 2.0: High complexity factor indicates significant acceleration/deceleration losses. Consider optimizing part geometry for fewer tight radii or evaluating if CAM feed rate optimization could reduce effective complexity.
Scrap/recut allowance > 15%: High scrap rate indicates process instability or quality issues. Review cutting parameters, material quality, nozzle condition, and machine calibration before production.

ISO 9013 Fine quality required for material > 6mm: Achieving fine quality (Rz ≤ 40µm for thin sheet) becomes increasingly difficult above 6mm. Verify that achievable with current laser power and gas selection. Consider reducing quality class for thicker materials.

fmea.effect: Laser operating well below rated power.
High risk of excessive heat input and dross formation.

fmea.failureMode: Gas cost > 30% of total job cost
fmea.effect: Gas consumption is a dominant cost driver.

error_propagation.method: GUM ISO/IEC Guide 98-3:2008 — LPU first-order Taylor expansion

reference_code.validation: Zod schema. Fail closed on zero/negative feed rate, zero rated power, negative costs, unverified safety gate.
reference_code.testing: Jest cases: thin sheet fiber cutting time (1mm mild steel), thick plate CO2 cutting time (12mm), nitrogen vs oxygen gas cost comparison, scrap allowance effect on total cost, OEM nozzle table vs measured flow comparison, safety gate block on unverified, power utilization low warning.
implementation_notes: Do not use eval(). Use existing AST expression engine.
Run formulas F1-F28 in deterministic order.
All time values in minutes internally; convert hours for display.
Gas flow rate precedence: MEASURED_FLOW > OEM_NOZZLE_TABLE > CALIBRATED_NOZZLE_PRESSURE_ESTIMATE.
Power utilization ratio is a diagnostic KPIs only — does not affect calculations.
Machine cost, gas cost, energy cost, and consumable cost summed for total job cost.
Scrap/recut allowance factor applies to all variable elements (time, gas, consumables).
All gate outputs are boolean. Use null for unavailable values, not Infinity.
Uncertainty propagation: combine time, gas flow, and cost uncertainties via RSS for each cost component.

metadata.description: Laser Cutting Time and Assist Gas Cost Calculator — Sheet Metal Laser Process Time, Gas Consumption and Cost Check per ISO 9013, ISO 11145, and ISO 14955-1. Computes effective feed rates, cycle times, assist gas consumption (via measured flow, OEM nozzle table, or calibrated pressure estimate), machine cost, energy cost, consumable cost, and total job cost for sheet metal laser cutting operations.
tuv_checklist: ISO 9013 thermal cutting quality classification confirmed
ISO 11145 laser gas flow measurement specification verified
ISO 14955-1 machine tool energy efficiency methodology confirmed
ISO 11553-1 laser processing machine safety audit reviewed
IEC 60825-1 laser product safety verification confirmed
Machine OEM cutting database parameter correlation checked
Power utilization ratio assessed per ISO 14955-1
Cost uncertainty propagation per ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 GUM verified
metadata.calibration_trace: Feed rate and time references from CAM system and machine control logs. Gas flow calibration traceable to thermal mass flow meter standards (ISO 5167-1). Pressure transducer calibration per IEC 61298-2 Class 1.0. Uncertainty propagation per ISO/IEC Guide 98-3:2008 GUM.
